name: Georgia Dietitian License
description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Georgia - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Board of Examiners of Licensed Dietitians

address.description:
Georgia Board of Examiners of Licensed Dietitians
237 Coliseum Drive
Macon, Georgia 31217-3858

renewalCycle: Every 2 years (by March 31, even years) + 30 CE hours

requiredDocuments.name: Official College Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Must be sent directly from the degree-granting institution, showing degree and date awarded.

requiredDocuments.name: CDR Registration Card or Award Letter
requiredDocuments.description: Copy of your current, active Commission on Dietetic Registration credential.

requiredDocuments.name: ACEND Verification Statement
requiredDocuments.description: Verifying completion of supervised practice experience.

requiredDocuments.name: Secure & Verifiable Document and Affidavit of Citizenship
requiredDocuments.description: As required by Georgia law for professional licensing.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Georgia dietitian license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The initial application fee is $85 (including a $10 processing fee). If you also request a provisional permit, add $25 for a total of $110. The biennial renewal fee is $60, due by March 31st of even-numbered years.
The Georgia Board of Examiners of Licensed Dietitians, which operates under the Georgia Secretary of State, is the regulatory body for dietitian licensure in the state.

mainEntity.name: What are the continuing education requirements for Georgia dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: You must complete 30 clock hours of continuing education during each two-year renewal cycle. As of 2026, all CE must be tracked and reported through CE Broker.

mainEntity.name: Does Georgia have reciprocity for dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: Georgia has enacted the Dietitian Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, dietitians still apply by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Georgia?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 15 to 20 Business Days.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Georgia while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
